shared_mechanism: Ehrhart-type lattice-point counting in integer dilates of an integral convex polytope, identified with the Hilbert polynomial giving dim H^0(X, L^{⊗r}) of tensor powers of an ample line bundle on the associated projective toric variety
brief_reason: For a projective toric variety built from a lattice polytope P, the number of lattice points in the r-th dilate rP equals the dimension of the space of global sections of the r-th power of its ample line bundle and is a polynomial in r (the Hilbert polynomial) — the same dilated-polytope lattice-point-counting machinery that makes the higher-spin alternating sign matrices the integer points of the r-th dilate of the ASM polytope, enumerated by an Ehrhart polynomial in r.
